text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions, to ensure each part works correctly in isolation. Integration tests go further, verifying that different parts of the system work well together. Many developers also use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps guide the design and catch errors early. Overall, thorough testing builds confidence in the software. When tests pass consistently, developers feel more comfortable making changes, adding new features, or fixing bugs, knowing that existing functionality remains stable and reliable.
